description.en: Flagship cut-flower crop of Sri Lanka's floriculture industry. ~10 ha under cultivation producing ~3 million flowers/year (mostly domestic market). **20+ species cultivated in Sri Lanka**; popular hues include red, pink, and the rare/expensive purple (in demand in Nuwara Eliya, Badulla, Kandy). White *A. andraeanum* is favoured for funerals, weddings, and corporate events. Commercial production under green houses, poly-tunnels, or netting. Tissue-culture labs (one specialised Sri Lankan facility) supply large volumes of planting material. Imported hybrid stock sourced from Anthura bv (Netherlands).

elevation_m.quote: Anthuriums can be grown at elevations up to 1500 metres above sea level, with texture and the flossiness remarkably enhanced with increase in elevation

description.quote: Annual production of anthuriums is around 3 million flowers, the majority of which are sold at the local market. A land area of approximately 10 hectares is under Anthurium cultivation at present. There are over 20 species of anthuriums in Sri Lanka.
